name: Accordion.types
visual_summary: An accordion represents multiple sections of content, and the component displays one section at a time. It is commonly used to avoid clutter and provide a clear hierarchy of information. Typically, an accordion has a header and a collapsible content area.
generation_prompt:
Create a production-ready accordion component with the following characteristics:

Name: Accordion.types
Description: An accordion represents multiple sections of content, and the component displays one section at a time. It is commonly used to avoid clutter and provide a clear hierarchy of information. Typically, an accordion has a header and a collapsible content area.
Style: compact, responsive
Interactions: clickable
Tags: Accordion, collapsible, nested

Requirements:
- Works in React with TypeScript by default but adaptable to Vue, Svelte, or plain HTML/CSS
- Uses Tailwind CSS for styling
- Fully accessible with ARIA labels and keyboard navigation
- Responsive and mobile-friendly
- No external dependencies beyond React and Tailwind unless necessary
- Include all necessary imports
- Export as default with sensible prop types

Generate clean production-ready code only. No explanations.
Reference implementation (adapt don't copy):
// packages/@mantine/core/src/components/Accordion/Accordion.types.ts
export type AccordionValue<Multiple extends boolean> = Multiple extends true
  ? string[]
  : string | null;

export type AccordionHeadingOrder = 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6;
export type AccordionChevronPosition = 'left' | 'right';
